column1: What does lower do to a string?
column2: It returns a new string with all letters lowercase

column1: What does upper do to a string?
column2: It returns a new string with all letters uppercase

column1: What does strip do to a string?
column2: It returns a new string with whitespace removed from the beginning and end

column1: What does title do to a string?
column2: It returns a new string with the first letter of each word capitalized

column1: Do string methods change the original string automatically?
column2: No they return a new string

column1: How do you keep the result of a string method?
column2: Store the result in a variable

column1: Why does answer.lower not permanently change answer?
column2: Because the returned string was not stored

column1: What does answer = answer.lower do?
column2: It stores the lowercase version back into answer

column1: What does strip remove?
column2: Whitespace from the beginning and end of a string

column1: Does strip remove spaces from the middle of a string?
column2: No it only removes whitespace from the beginning and end

column1: What does method chaining mean?
column2: Calling multiple methods one after another

column1: What does answer.strip().lower do?
column2: It removes outer whitespace then converts the result to lowercase

column1: Which method runs first in answer.strip().lower?
column2: strip runs first

column1: Which method runs second in answer.strip().lower?
column2: lower runs second

column1: What does normalizing input mean?
column2: Converting user input into a consistent format before checking it

column1: Why normalize user input?
column2: To make comparisons more reliable

column1: How should the Library app clean a yes or no response?
column2: Use strip and lower

column1: How should the Library app format a reader name?
column2: Use strip and title

column1: What should Yes become after lower?
column2: yes

column1: What should space yes space become after strip?
column2: yes

column1: What should space YES space become after strip and lower?
column2: yes

column1: Why did Yes fail before Lesson 006?
column2: Because string comparisons are exact and Yes does not equal yes

column1: What was the project goal for Lesson 006?
column2: Normalize user input before checking it
